title: amaryllis belladonna subsp. haywardii
intro: Amaryllis belladonna subsp. haywardii, also known as the Cape Belladonna, is a subspecies of the Amaryllis belladonna plant, a bulbous perennial native to South Africa. The plant is used medicinally due to its alkaloid content, particularly the belladonna alkaloids, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Amaryllis belladonna subsp. haywardii include its ability to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and act as an antispasmodic and anticholinergic agent, making it useful for treating conditions such as muscle spasms, epilepsy, and Parkinson's disease. Amaryllis belladonna subsp. haywardii can be prepared as an herbal remedy, typically through drying and processing the plant's leaves and roots to be used in teas, tinctures, or ointments.
